rationale: The pair is not an obvious incidental mention: the citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior precommitment work on timing of choices, and the cited paper is a field-experimental study of advance (delayed) ordering affecting healthier choices—conceptually aligned with that prior stream. Given this strong construct overlap (precommitment/advance choice delay) and no in-text evidence showing it is merely perfunctory, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
